design_principles: P1: Simple language. A tiny LLM needs CLARITY over PRECISION.
P2: No internal mechanics (formulas, multipliers, balance constants).
P3: No flavor or lore. Fragments are CONTEXT, not worldbuilding.
P4: Be explicit about what game terms MEAN for narration.
P5: If the developer doesn't immediately understand it, rewrite it.
P6: Every sentence should help the LLM DISTINGUISH this event from others.

writing_guide.tone: Factual, declarative. No questions, no speculation.
writing_guide.test: Would removing this fragment cause the LLM to misnarrate? If no, remove it.
writing_guide.edge_cases: Add a fragment when two events look the same but mean different things.

auto_generation.note: Future: new entities auto-generate fragments via LLM, using _core + relevant domain + entity game data as meta-prompt context.

_core: You narrate events in an open world RPG. The world uses a 4 tier classification system: T1 (common, early-game) through T4 (legendary, end-game). Players fight enemies, gather resources from resource nodes, craft items across 6 crafting disciplines, and explore a procedurally generated world with forests, caves, quarries, and villages.
_output:
Respond with JSON only. Two fields:
- "narrative": ONE factual sentence. Mention count and location if regional. Note patterns like concentrated activity, sustained effort, or milestones. Do not editorialize.
- "tags": Assign applicable tags from this list (only include what clearly fits):
  significance: minor, moderate, significant, major, critical

Example: {"narrative": "Player has killed 25 wolves in Whispering Woods, a sustained culling effort.", "tags": ["significance:significant"]}
domain:combat: This is a combat event. Players fight enemies using melee weapons, ranged weapons, and skills. Damage types include physical, fire, ice, lightning, poison, arcane, shadow, holy. Enemies have tiers (T1-T4) indicating difficulty.
domain:gathering: This is a resource gathering event. Players harvest from world nodes using tools (pickaxe for ore, axe for wood, hammer for stone). Nodes can be depleted and regenerate over time.
domain:crafting: This is a crafting event. Six disciplines: smithing (weapons/armor), alchemy (potions), refining (processing raw materials), engineering (devices), enchanting (magical properties), fishing. Quality ranges from normal to legendary.
domain:progression: This is a progression event. Max level 30 with slightly exponential EXP curve. Six stats: STR (melee damage), DEF (damage reduction), VIT (health), LCK (crit chance), AGI (speed), INT (magic/mana). 40+ titles earned through gameplay milestones.
domain:exploration: This is an exploration event. The world is divided into regions of increasing danger. Biomes include forest, cave, quarry, and water. Dungeons are structured encounters with enemy waves.
domain:social: This is a social event. NPCs provide quests and dialogue. Quest types include kill, gather, craft, explore, and talk objectives.
domain:economy: This is an economy event. Gold earned from quests, loot, and trading. Gold spent on skill unlocks, repairs, and purchases.
domain:skills: This is a skill usage event. 30+ skills with mana costs and cooldowns. Skills include combat strikes, elemental spells, and gathering buffs.
domain:items: This is an item event. Equipment includes weapons, armor, tools, and consumables. Items have tier and durability. At 0% durability items work at half effectiveness but never break.
domain:dungeon: This is a dungeon event. Dungeons are structured areas with enemy waves and a completion objective. Different from open-world combat which is unstructured and emergent.
species:wolf_grey: Grey Wolf is a tier 1 enemy found in forest biomes. Low individual threat.
species:wolf_dire: Dire Wolf is a tier 2 enemy found in forest biomes. Moderate threat.
species:wolf_elder: Elder Wolf is a tier 3 enemy found in deep forest areas. Dangerous.
species:slime_green: Green Slime is a tier 1 enemy found in caves and wet areas. Low individual threat.
species:slime_acid: Acid Slime is a tier 2 enemy found in caves and wet areas. Moderate threat.
species:slime_crystal: Crystal Slime is a tier 3 enemy found in cave depths. Dangerous.
species:beetle_brown: Brown Beetle is a tier 1 enemy found in underground areas. Low individual threat.
species:beetle_armored: Armored Beetle is a tier 2 enemy found in underground areas. Moderate threat.
species:beetle_titan: Titan Beetle is a tier 4 enemy found in deep underground. Extremely dangerous.
species:golem_stone: Stone Golem is a tier 3 enemy found in quarry and cave biomes. Dangerous.
species:golem_crystal: Crystal Golem is a tier 4 enemy found in quarry depths. Extremely dangerous.
species:void_wraith: Void Wraith is a tier 4 enemy found in corrupted end-game areas. Extremely dangerous.
species:entity_primordial: Primordial Entity is a tier 4 enemy found in deep end-game areas. Extremely dangerous.
material_category:metal: Metal ores and ingots. Mined with pickaxes from ore veins. Primary materials for smithing weapons and armor.
material_category:wood: Wood from trees. Chopped with axes. Used for handles, engineering, and construction.
material_category:stone: Stone and gems. Mined from quarry nodes. Used in refining and engineering.
material_category:elemental: Elemental materials with magical properties. Found in special nodes or dropped by enemies. Used in enchanting and alchemy.
material_category:monster_drop: Materials dropped by defeated enemies. Tier scales with enemy tier. Used across multiple crafting disciplines.
discipline:smithing: Smithing produces weapons and armor from metals at anvil stations.
discipline:alchemy: Alchemy produces potions and consumables by combining reagents at cauldron stations.
discipline:refining: Refining processes raw materials into usable components. Converts ores to ingots, logs to planks.
discipline:engineering: Engineering produces mechanical devices, turrets, traps, and bombs from diverse materials.
discipline:enchanting: Enchanting adds magical properties to equipment such as damage bonuses, protection, and lifesteal.
discipline:fishing: Fishing catches fish from water nodes using rods. Catches vary by location.
tier:1: Tier 1 is common, early-game content. Routine for experienced players.
tier:2: Tier 2 is uncommon, mid-game content. Requires some player investment to handle.
tier:3: Tier 3 is rare, late-game content. Significant challenge and reward. Noteworthy activity.
tier:4: Tier 4 is legendary, end-game content. Extremely rare. Any T4 activity is a major event worth highlighting.
element:physical: Physical damage. The default melee and ranged type.
element:fire: Fire damage. Can apply burn (damage over time).
element:ice: Ice damage. Can apply chill or freeze (slows or immobilizes).
element:lightning: Lightning damage. Can chain to nearby targets.
element:poison: Poison damage. Applies damage over time that stacks.
element:arcane: Arcane damage. Pure magical energy, rarely resisted.
element:holy: Holy damage. Effective against undead and shadow creatures.
rank:normal: Normal rank. Standard encounter, common in the world.
rank:boss: Boss rank. Rare and powerful. Defeating one is a notable milestone.
rank:unique: Unique rank. One-of-a-kind encounter. Major world event.
status_effect:burn: Burn deals fire damage over time.
status_effect:bleed: Bleed deals physical damage over time.
status_effect:poison: Poison deals damage over time, can stack.
status_effect:chill: Chill slows movement and attack speed.
status_effect:root: Root prevents movement but allows attacks.
status_effect:shock: Shock interrupts and deals burst damage.
status_effect:weaken: Weaken reduces the target's damage output.
status_effect:haste: Haste increases movement and attack speed.
status_effect:encumbered: Encumbered from heavy inventory, reduces speed.
action:deplete: Node depletion means the resource source is fully harvested and temporarily unavailable. Different from normal gathering which just collects some resources.
action:invent: Invented items are player-created combinations not found in any recipe book. This is a creative achievement, rarer and more noteworthy than standard recipe crafting.
result:first: This is the first time this specific action has occurred.
result:occurrence: This is the Nth time this specific action has occurred.
result:critical: This was a critical hit, dealing extra damage.
result:perfect: This was a perfect execution, highest possible quality.
result:first_try: This succeeded on the first attempt.
result:near_death: The player nearly died during this activity.
result:no_damage: The player completed this without taking any damage.
result:record: This set a new personal record.
domain:barriers: Barrier placement and pickup. Barriers are player-placed obstacles from stone materials.
domain:encyclopedia: Encyclopedia discovery. Players unlock entries by encountering new enemies, items, and recipes.
domain:records: Personal record tracking. Fastest times, highest damage, longest streaks.
domain:time: Time tracking. How long the player spends in different activities.
domain:session: Session tracking. Login/logout and play session duration.
domain:misc: Miscellaneous activity. Menu usage, saves, debug actions.
material_category:fish: Fish caught from water nodes using fishing rods.
material_category:ore: Ores mined from veins with pickaxes. Smelted into metals for smithing.
material_category:plant: Plants gathered by hand. Used in alchemy for potions.
material_category:tree: Wood chopped from trees with axes. Used for handles and engineering.
quality:masterwork: Masterwork quality. Top 10% crafting performance. Noteworthy achievement.
quality:legendary: Legendary quality. Near-perfect crafting performance. Extremely rare outcome.
rarity:rare: Rare rarity. Notable find or encounter.
attack_type:melee: Melee combat. Close-range weapons like swords, axes, daggers, maces.
attack_type:ranged: Ranged combat. Bows and thrown weapons, attacking from distance.
attack_type:magic: Magic combat. Elemental spells using mana.
attack_type:critical: Critical hit. Bonus damage based on LCK stat.
attack_type:aoe: Area of effect attack. Hits multiple targets in a radius.
attack_type:chain: Chain attack. Damage jumps between multiple targets.
item_category:weapon: Weapons. Swords, axes, bows, daggers, maces, hammers, staffs.
item_category:consumable: Consumable items. Used once for an immediate effect.
item_category:tool: Tools. Pickaxes, axes, hammers used for gathering resources.
item_category:device: Engineered devices. Turrets, traps, bombs from engineering discipline.
item_category:potion: Potions. Healing, mana restoration, or buff effects from alchemy.
item_category:bomb: Bombs. Throwable explosive devices from engineering.
item_category:trap: Traps. Placed on ground to damage enemies who walk over them.
item_category:turret: Turrets. Stationary devices that auto-attack nearby enemies.
tool:pickaxe: Pickaxe. Used to mine ore and stone from veins and quarry nodes.
tool:axe: Axe. Used to chop trees for wood.
tool:hammer: Hammer. Used to mine stone and break rocks.
tool:fishing_rod: Fishing rod. Used to catch fish from water nodes.
npc:combat_trainer: Combat Trainer. Teaches combat skills and offers fight-related quests.
npc:mysterious_trader: Mysterious Trader. Sells rare items and offers unusual trades.
npc:tutorial_guide: Tutorial Guide. Introduces game mechanics to new players.
source:combat: Earned from combat activities (killing enemies, dungeon rewards).
source:loot: Earned from loot drops and chest contents.
resource:exp: Experience points. Accumulated toward level progression.
